question_text: A car has a mass of 1000 kg. The friction force acting on it is 5000 N. What is the size of the normal force pressing the car onto the ground?
explanation: The normal force is the weight of the car pushing down on the ground. Weight = mass × gravity, so 1000 kg × 10 N/kg = 10,000 N. This is the force pressing the surfaces together, which affects the friction.
